agent_profiles.role_description: Star Alliance anchor carrier with dominant Newark hub; largest US carrier on transatlantic routes by departures
agent_profiles.market_position: Market leader on US-Europe O&D with ~22% capacity share
resource_constraints: Aging widebody fleet requiring CapEx ($2.1B 787/777X orders outstanding)
competitive_advantages: Deepest Star Alliance network (56 partners) enabling seamless connection revenue
Polaris business class rated top-tier on premium routes
Strong corporate travel contracts (Fortune 500 concentration in NYC/Chicago)
behavioral_traits: Defend existing slot portfolio aggressively — loss averse on LHR slots
Leverage Star Alliance depth before bidding unilateral capacity
Respond to Delta pricing moves within 48 hours on competitive O&Ds

decision_variables.name: slot_bid_value
decision_variables.range: [0, 150] million USD per slot pair per season
decision_variables.description: Amount bid in IATA slot auction for each additional slot pair at hub airports

decision_variables.name: capacity_allocation
decision_variables.range: [0, 100] percent change vs baseline weekly frequencies
decision_variables.description: Weekly seat capacity deployed on each transatlantic route cluster

decision_variables.name: business_class_price
decision_variables.description: Yield management anchor price for business cabin on transatlantic routes

decision_variables.name: economy_price
decision_variables.description: Base economy fare; drives load factor and connecting traffic stimulation

decision_variables.name: alliance_offer
decision_variables.description: Level of commercial coordination offered to alliance partners this round

decision_variables.name: fleet_deployment
decision_variables.description: Widebody aircraft type assigned to transatlantic rotation

decision_variables.name: lounge_investment
decision_variables.description: Whether to invest in hub lounge upgrade this round (improves premium yield next round)

decision_variables.name: regulatory_lobby
decision_variables.description: Whether to activate regulatory lobbying to block competitor slot applications

objectives.name: revenue_per_asm
objectives.description: Revenue per available seat mile — primary profitability metric for TATL operations

objectives.name: slot_portfolio_value
objectives.description: Strategic value of slot holdings at Level 3 airports (LHR, JFK, FRA)

objectives.name: market_share
objectives.description: Percentage share of North Atlantic O&D passengers

objectives.name: premium_cabin_yield
objectives.description: Average revenue per business/first class seat — key driver of total profitability

objectives.name: operating_cost
objectives.description: Cost per available seat mile (CASM) — efficiency metric

constraints: Maximum slot acquisition capped at regulatory limits under EU-US Open Skies Agreement
Heathrow Movement Cap: no carrier may file operations that would push LHR above 480,000 annual ATMs
Alliance anti-trust immunity: Star Alliance (UA/LH) and SkyTeam (DL/AF) JV partners may not set prices independently on immunised routes

agent_profiles.role_description: SkyTeam lead carrier aggressively expanding transatlantic premium capacity via JFK hub build-out and Air France-KLM partnership deepening
agent_profiles.market_position: Strong #2 with fastest premium cabin growth 2022-2025
resource_constraints: JFK Terminal 4 capacity ceiling limiting widebody rotations
SkyTeam integration costs with Air France-KLM post-COVID restructuring
High hedging losses in 2022-2023 reducing available capital
competitive_advantages: Delta One business suite rated #1 by multiple surveys — pricing power +12% vs peers
Fastest fleet renewal (A321XLR deliveries accelerating narrow-body TATL)
Highest on-time performance metric in industry (2022-2024)
behavioral_traits: Undercut incumbents on leisure routes to capture market share then reprice
Deepen Air France-KLM JV to create de facto merged network across Atlantic
Invest in differentiated premium product to justify premium yield

Delta One premium demand forecast by corridor (proprietary)

Corporate RFP win rates vs United on key accounts

agent_profiles.role_description: IAG flagship carrier leveraging Heathrow dominance and Club World premium brand for premium-heavy transatlantic revenue model
agent_profiles.market_position: Heathrow slot incumbent with 40%+ capacity share at LHR T5
resource_constraints: Heathrow expansion blocked — LHR capped at 480k movements/year
IAG balance sheet recovery post-COVID (net debt still elevated)
Legacy pension obligations constraining labour flexibility
competitive_advantages: Largest LHR slot portfolio — irreplaceable strategic asset
oneworld premium network (AA, Iberia, Finnair) enabling premium through-connections
Club World suite and Concorde Room lounges as premium differentiators
behavioral_traits: Prioritise premium yield protection over volume — will sacrifice load factor for RASM
Lobby regulators aggressively to defend LHR slot grandfather rights
Resist slot give-ups — any slot lost is permanent competitive disadvantage

agent_profiles.role_description: Star Alliance co-anchor with dual Frankfurt/Munich hub strategy and largest European widebody fleet
agent_profiles.market_position: Dominant European legacy carrier with 18% transatlantic capacity share
resource_constraints: German labour relations (ver.di/Cockpit) create strike risk and cost rigidity
Dual hub complexity (FRA + MUC) increasing fixed costs vs single-hub peers
EU state aid conditions from 2020 bailout restricting some expansion
competitive_advantages: Business Class Senator Lounge network strongest in Europe
Deep cargo network on widebodies cross-subsidises passenger yield
Swiss/Austrian/Brussels Airlines feeder traffic reduces seat-mile cost
behavioral_traits: Defensive on Frankfurt slots — treat FRA dominance as non-negotiable
Coordinate with United via Star Alliance JV before making solo capacity decisions
Systematic, analytical decision process — slower to react but rarely makes strategic errors

agent_profiles.role_description: SkyTeam co-anchor at CDG with strong leisure/VFR traffic base but lagging premium cabin product vs peers; post-COVID restructuring constraining growth
agent_profiles.market_position: 4th largest carrier on North Atlantic by RASM; CDG hub strong but not slot-constrained
resource_constraints: French labour regulations (CGT) creating highest CASM among the five (~15.8 cents)
La Premiere first-class niche product limits total addressable premium revenue
French government 18% ownership creates political constraints on restructuring
competitive_advantages: CDG uncongested vs LHR/JFK — easier to add frequencies on demand
Air France-KLM JV with Delta creates de facto merged network
Strongest leisure/VFR corridor to French-speaking markets (West Africa, Caribbean)
behavioral_traits: Follow Delta's lead on alliance and pricing decisions before acting independently
Protect French government-sensitive routes (CDG-Montreal, CDG-New York) politically
React to competitor capacity additions within 2-3 rounds rather than anticipating
